steps.title: Spot the distribution that would support the assertion
steps.content: The assertion claims four hot Jupiters ($\text{mass} > 0.25$, $\text{period} < 10$ days) and one warm Jupiter ($\text{mass} > 0.25$, $\text{period} > 10$ days). Supporting data must show exactly one planet with orbital period exceeding 10 days and all five planets with mass greater than 0.25.

steps.title: Match the data distribution to the correct choice
steps.content:
Choice D states that all planets have mass greater than 0.25 Jupiters and all except TOI-1478 b have periods less than 10 days.

Examining the table: all masses range from 0.85 to 6.33 Jupiters (meeting the threshold), TOI-1478 b has a period of 10.180 days (the only one exceeding 10), and the other four have periods of 5.003, 5.331, 3.409, and 4.720 days (all below 10).

Choices A, B, and C either claim no planet exceeds 10 days, misread the period data, or name the wrong planet.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
